steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures at the blank
steps.content: The left side is an independent clause ending with “supernova” as the object. The right side is a noun phrase (“the explosion of a massive star”) containing an internal relative clause (“which blasts...”). The right side elaborates on and renames the preceding noun.

steps.title: Apply the enclosure rule for appositives
steps.content:
An appositive must be set off by matching punctuation on both sides. Option D correctly frames the appositive with em dashes—opening before “the” and closing after “space.” The other three fail: option A's colon and option B's comma don't properly enclose appositives, and option C's period breaks the sentence.
This matches choice D — D is correct.
